defs: A body of heavy-armed infantry formed in ranks and files close and deep. There were several different arrangements, the phalanx varying in depth from four to twenty-five or more ranks of men.
quotes: The Grecian phalanx, moveless as a tower.

defs: Any body of troops or men formed in close array, or any combination of people distinguished for firmness and solidity of a union.
quotes: At present they formed a united phalanx.
The sheep recumbent, and the sheep that grazed, All huddling into phalanx, stood and gazed.

defs: One of the digital bones of the hand or foot, beyond the metacarpus or metatarsus; an internode.
A group or bundle of stamens, as in polyadelphous flowers.
